query: I've got a 300-file Rust monorepo and need to produce per-crate ownership summaries plus a dependency cycle report. Decompose this with RLM, fan out per crate, reconcile back. Budget is tight.
ok so this rust workspace has like 60 crates and every time i try to analyse it claude eats the context, can you split the work into sandboxed sub-queries and stitch the answers back together
Use terraphim_rlm to run this untrusted scraper script in isolation -- I don't trust the input HTML and don't want it touching my machine outside a VM.
Spawn a python sandbox via terraphim_rlm to process this 4GB JSONL log file and produce a histogram of error codes per service. Track the budget.
/rlm decompose this refactor: extract the auth middleware into its own crate, update 17 call sites, run cargo test, then add an ADR.
I need recursive LLM calls -- the outer model should plan, then call cheaper sub-models for each step, then a final reconciliation pass. Use whatever Terraphim has for this.
Long task: build a knowledge graph from 200 Markdown research notes by extracting entities per file in parallel, then merging. Sandbox the python so it can't write outside a scratch dir.
Wrap this overnight analysis in a budget-tracked recursive session -- if it goes over 50k tokens I want it to stop and ask, not silently truncate.
Add a TODO comment on line 42 of src/lib.rs that says 'fixme: bounds check'.
What's the difference between `Arc<Mutex<T>>` and `Arc<RwLock<T>>` in tokio? Two paragraphs is fine.
Open the README.md and find the install instructions. Quote them back to me.
Review this PR diff for security issues -- it's about 200 lines touching JWT validation.
Search my Rust personal notes for any prior work on tokio cancellation tokens.
Generate a 5-line bash one-liner that finds all *.lock files modified in the last 24 hours and prints them sorted by size.
Kick off the meta-learning agent on bigbox to run overnight, I'll check the status in the morning.
Ingest the conclusions from this debugging session into my Rust Engineer knowledge graph so I can find them later.
Format this Rust file with rustfmt and tell me what changed.
Run this Python script and tell me the exit code: `python -c 'print(2+2)'`
I want to compare three reviewer perspectives -- security, performance, correctness -- on this big migration PR. Adversarial swarm please.
How do I write an integration test in tokio that mocks an HTTP server? Show me the boilerplate.
